name: crm_hybrid_customer_plus_inherited
description:
Pins the CRM hybrid-prepend NATURAL-FALL-THROUGH path at engine/assessor.py:752-763 — the case where dual-scope responsibilities mix in a way that does NOT satisfy `all(r in _CRM_SHORT_CIRCUIT_SET for r in specified)` at :741, so the short-circuit declines and execution falls through to the LLM with the responsibility-split block prepended.

cloud='customer' + onprem='inherited':
  - 'customer' is NOT in _CRM_SHORT_CIRCUIT_SET (only provider/inherited/not_applicable are) — so the all() check at :741 fails, the short-circuit at :743 is skipped.
  - cloud_r is populated AND onprem_r is populated AND cloud_r != onprem_r → needs_hybrid_block=True at :752-756.
  - hybrid block is prepended to tagged_evidence at :758-763 and the assessor proceeds into the normal LLM pipeline.

Distinct from test_force_llm_keeps_hybrid_crm_prepend at test_eval_harness.py:414-474 which pins the SAME prepend invariant but with force_llm=True. The kernel-invariant test proves force_llm doesn't BYPASS the prepend; this case proves the prepend FIRES in the default (no force_llm) flow when the conditions are right. Together they pin both sides of the gate.

Also the first eval case to exercise the new llm_prompt_evidence_contains_regex expected key in the runner — asserts on stub.calls[0]['tagged_evidence'] to verify the literal '## responsibility_split' header reaches the LLM prompt. Decision-only assertions (status/source/llm_calls) can't distinguish 'the LLM saw the prepend' from 'the LLM happened to land on the right verdict without it' — this key forces the prompt-shape invariant explicitly.

Control SI-4 (Information System Monitoring) is a realistic dual-scope hybrid CRM pattern: the IaaS customer must configure tenant-side telemetry (CloudTrail / CloudWatch / VPC flow logs) because the provider does NOT monitor the contents of tenant accounts — only the underlying infrastructure. On-prem is inherited from the enterprise SOC that already has 24x7 coverage of the boundary egress. The customer therefore has a real assessable cloud-side responsibility that the LLM must reason about, while the on-prem half is dispatched by the inheritance reference.

stub proposal carries a Compliant narrative with the affirming phrase 'confirmed via' and a USD doc citation so the validator accepts it on the first pass (per feedback_eval_compliant_narrative_must_affirm — without an affirming phrase the validator would reject → retry → stub queue exhaustion). source_baseline_id=999 matches the sentinel used across the CRM corpus so failure messages stay diff-able.

ccis_row.guidance: The organization monitors the information system to detect attacks and indicators of potential attacks.
ccis_row.procedures: Examine continuous monitoring tooling and SOC processes; confirm telemetry from both cloud-tenant and on-prem boundary feeds the detection pipeline.

tagged_evidence:
## Tagged evidence
- USD00088888 Cloud Telemetry Configuration Standard Rev D — documents the tenant-side CloudTrail / CloudWatch / VPC flow log configuration the customer owns.

crm_entries.narrative: Tenant-side monitoring (CloudTrail, CloudWatch metric filters, VPC flow logs) is the customer's responsibility; the IaaS provider does not inspect tenant account contents.
crm_entries.narrative_onprem: On-prem monitoring is inherited from the enterprise SOC that already operates 24x7 boundary-egress detection across the parent system boundary.

llm_stub_proposals.narrative: Examined the customer-side cloud monitoring posture; confirmed via USD00088888 that tenant CloudTrail, CloudWatch metric filters, and VPC flow logs are configured to feed the detection pipeline for the customer-owned scope.
